_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: Nobody loves him. Everybody needs him. He's made his peace with which one matters.

museum_objects.title: Original Crude Oil Sample from the Drake Well
museum_objects.description: The only surviving original crude oil sample from the world's first commercial oil well, drilled by Edwin Drake.
museum_objects.significance: This is the actual liquid that started it all — before pipelines, before OPEC, before a single barrel was ever priced, this was just oil bubbling out of the ground in Pennsylvania.
museum_objects.plain_language: The actual surviving sample of oil from the very first commercial oil well ever drilled, in 1859.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: John A. Mather Oil Industry Photographs
museum_objects.description: A photographic archive documenting the earliest days of the Pennsylvania oil boom by pioneering photographer John A. Mather.
museum_objects.significance: These photos are the only visual record of the exact moment the world realized oil could be an industry, not just a curiosity.
museum_objects.plain_language: Old photographs documenting the very first oil boom in Pennsylvania during the 1860s.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: First Oil Well Photograph
museum_objects.description: An early photograph of the Drake oil well site, part of the Library of Congress's historical photo collection.
museum_objects.significance: A single black-and-white image of a wooden derrick — the modest, unglamorous birthplace of the industry that would eventually run the world.
museum_objects.plain_language: An old photo of the very first oil well, showing how modest and small the birthplace of the oil industry really was.

display_alternatives.title: Oil well in construction in Bashkortostan, Russia.png

museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. We cite one and show the other, and we say so. | IMAGE QA FAIL: The image shows contemporary oil extraction equipment in a desert, not an early photograph of the Drake oil well site.

museum_objects.title: OPEC Founding Treaty Document
museum_objects.description: The founding treaty establishing the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries.
museum_objects.significance: This is the moment Zane stopped being controlled by a handful of American companies and started being fought over by entire nations.
museum_objects.plain_language: The actual founding treaty of OPEC, the organization of oil-producing countries that changed who controls oil prices.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: Samuel Kier's Rock Oil Advertisement
museum_objects.description: An early advertisement by Samuel Kier marketing petroleum as a medicinal 'rock oil' before its fuel value was understood.
museum_objects.significance: Before anyone knew oil would fuel the modern world, someone was trying to sell it as medicine. Zane's whole identity started as a misunderstanding.
museum_objects.plain_language: An old advertisement selling oil as a medicine — years before anyone realized it would become the world's most important fuel.

world_map.map_description: Zane's crude oil market was born in a Pennsylvania forest and grew into a globe-spanning commodity fought over from Texas to the Middle East.

nodes.label: Titusville, Pennsylvania
nodes.description: Site of the Drake Well, the first commercial oil well in the United States, drilled in 1859.

nodes.label: Baku, Azerbaijan
nodes.description: One of the world's earliest and largest oil-producing regions, rivaling Pennsylvania in the 19th century.

nodes.label: Spindletop, Texas
nodes.description: Site of the 1901 gusher that launched the modern Texas oil boom and Gulf Coast refining industry.

nodes.label: Baghdad, Iraq
nodes.description: Region central to OPEC's founding and the geopolitics of Middle Eastern oil production.

nodes.label: Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
nodes.description: Early refining and distribution hub for Pennsylvania crude oil in the industry's first decades.
